message:
feat(indexer/responsiveness): expose machine resource policy on GovernorConfig + scale in-flight byte budgets

Extend the process-wide responsiveness governor to model the two
resource dimensions that matter for rebuild tuning — worker count
and in-flight bytes — consistently, with environment overrides and
policy-driven defaults.

New fields on `GovernorConfig`:
- `available_parallelism` — cached `std::thread::available_parallelism`
  (defaults to 1 on error) so downstream code doesn't have to re-query.
- `reserved_cores` — interactive-headroom floor. Default is
  `default_reserved_cores_for_available(n)` which scales with machine
  size (1/1/2/8 reserved on 1/2/8/64-core boxes) and is capped at
  `available_parallelism - 1` so we never reserve every core.
  Overridable via `CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_RESERVED_CORES`.
- `max_workers` — hard ceiling on worker pool size. Defaults to
  `worker_ceiling_for(available_parallelism, reserved_cores)` and is
  overridable via `CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_MAX_WORKERS`. Clamped to
  `>= 1` after the env override.
- `max_inflight_bytes` / `min_inflight_bytes` — 512 MiB ceiling /
  1 MiB floor on any producer-side byte budget the governor scales.
  `min` is clamped to `<= max` and `>= 1` so no combination of env
  overrides can produce an empty interval.

New public helpers:
- `effective_inflight_byte_limit(desired_bytes)` — mirrors the
  existing `effective_worker_count(desired)` shape. Applies the live
  capacity factor plus the configured ceilings via the new
  `scale_inflight_byte_limit` helper.
- `scale_worker_count_with_policy(desired, capacity_pct, &cfg)` —
  extends `scale_worker_count` to know about `reserved_cores` and
  `max_workers`, and now backs the public `effective_worker_count`
  by routing through the current `GovernorConfig`.

Policy invariants (pinned by tests):
- `default_reserved_core_policy_preserves_interactive_headroom` —
  1/2/8/64 cores → 0/1/2/8 reserved.
- `worker_policy_applies_reserved_cores_and_live_capacity` — a
  16-core box with 4 reserved and `max_workers=20` caps workers at
  12 at full capacity, scales to 6 at 50%, still returns 1 for
  tiny desired counts under pressure, and returns 0 for
  `desired=0`.
- `inflight_byte_policy_caps_and_scales_without_increasing_small_budgets` —
  a 512 MiB request clamps to `max_inflight_bytes` at full capacity,
  scales proportionally at 25%, never grows a 4 MiB request into
  something larger, and preserves `0 → 0` passthrough.
- Telemetry serialization test extended to assert the new
  `resource_policy`, `reserved_cores`, and `max_inflight_bytes`
  fields appear in the JSON.

Helper plumbing:
- `available_parallelism()` wraps `std::thread::available_parallelism`
  with a `.unwrap_or(1)` fallback.
- `env_usize(key)` sibling to the existing env-parse helpers.

No behavior change to call sites that don't opt in; downstream
producer-side integration lands in the lexical rebuild pipeline in
the companion commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
